phases.title: BLOCK 1 — POSITIONS & DRILLS
phases.subtitle: Weeks 1–4 · 6/11–7/6 · Establish positions, fix faults at the root

intent.title: Week 1 · 6/11–6/15
intent.text: Establish positions. Thursday: drill the clean rack (elbows forward, not down) and the jerk split (back heel up, toes forward). Monday: build pull speed through the knee and start locking out the snatch overhead.

exercises.name: Muscle Clean
exercises.note: Elbows PUNCH forward and UP from hip to rack — if your elbows are pointing down you have no rack · 45s rest

exercises.name: Pause Clean (3s in hole)
exercises.note: Feel your foot position in the hole — hip-width, parallel tracks, not sumo. Elbows high throughout. · 2 min rest

exercises.name: Clean + 3 Front Squats
exercises.note: Hold the front rack actively on all 3 squats — elbows forward, no dropping · 2 min rest

exercises.name: Split Jerk (3s hold in split)
exercises.note: BACK FOOT: heel UP, toes pointing forward — not sideways. Hold 3 seconds and check before standing. · 2 min rest

exercises.name: Front Squat
exercises.note: Active front rack every rep — elbows chase the wall in front of you · 2 min rest

exercises.name: Push Press
exercises.note: Reinforce overhead position — drives the elbow-up habit · 75s rest

exercises.name: Overhead Squat
exercises.note: Arms locked OUT before you descend — press the bar into the sky, don't let it sit on a bent arm · 90s rest

exercises.name: Muscle Snatch
exercises.note: Arms lock overhead BEFORE the bar stops moving — if you're catching bent, you turned over too slow · 60s rest

exercises.name: Hang Snatch (above knee)
exercises.note: Starting above the knee means you CANNOT stop at the knee — use this. Accelerate to the hip. · 2 min rest

exercises.name: Snatch Pull (no stop at knee)
exercises.note: Bar sweeps CONTINUOUSLY off the floor — no speed bump at the knee. Think "sweep to hip." · 90s rest

exercises.name: Back Squat
exercises.note: 2 min rest

exercises.name: Bent Over Row
exercises.note: Back strength for the pull · 75s rest

intent.title: Week 2 · 6/18–6/22
intent.text: Load climbs slightly. Maintain the technical focus from week 1 — speed through the knee on pulls, active front rack on all cleans, back foot check on every jerk.

exercises.note: Elbows PUNCH forward and UP from hip to rack — if your elbows are pointing down you have no rack · 45s rest

exercises.name: Pause Clean (3s in hole)

exercises.name: Clean + 3 Front Squats
exercises.note: Hold the front rack actively on all 3 squats — elbows forward, no dropping · 2 min rest

exercises.note: BACK FOOT: heel UP, toes pointing forward — not sideways. Hold 3 seconds and check before standing. · 2 min rest

exercises.name: Front Squat
exercises.note: Active front rack every rep — elbows chase the wall in front of you · 2 min rest

exercises.name: Push Press
exercises.note: Reinforce overhead position — drives the elbow-up habit · 75s rest

exercises.name: Overhead Squat
exercises.note: Arms locked OUT before you descend — press the bar into the sky, don't let it sit on a bent arm · 90s rest

exercises.name: Muscle Snatch
exercises.note: Arms lock overhead BEFORE the bar stops moving — if you're catching bent, you turned over too slow · 60s rest

exercises.name: Hang Snatch (above knee)
exercises.note: Starting above the knee means you CANNOT stop at the knee — use this. Accelerate to the hip. · 2 min rest

exercises.name: Snatch Pull (no stop at knee)
exercises.note: Bar sweeps CONTINUOUSLY off the floor — no speed bump at the knee. Think "sweep to hip." · 90s rest

exercises.name: Back Squat
exercises.note: 2 min rest

exercises.name: Bent Over Row
exercises.note: Back strength for the pull · 75s rest

intent.title: Week 3 · 6/25–6/29
intent.text: Peak loading week of the positional block. Heavier cleans and snatches — the technique habits must hold when the bar gets heavier.

exercises.name: Pause Clean (3s in hole)

exercises.name: Clean + 3 Front Squats

exercises.name: Muscle Snatch
exercises.note: Arms lock overhead BEFORE the bar stops moving — if you're catching bent, you turned over too slow · 60s rest

exercises.note: Starting above the knee means you CANNOT stop at the knee — use this. Accelerate to the hip. · 2 min rest

exercises.name: Back Squat
exercises.note: 2 min rest

exercises.name: Bent Over Row
exercises.note: Back strength for the pull · 75s rest

intent.title: Week 4 · 7/2–7/6
intent.text: Deload. Volume drops significantly. Keep the intensity on key positions but no grinding. Prep for the power block.

exercises.name: Muscle Clean
exercises.note: Elbows PUNCH forward and UP from hip to rack — if your elbows are pointing down you have no rack · 45s rest

exercises.name: Pause Clean (3s in hole)
exercises.note: Feel your foot position in the hole — hip-width, parallel tracks, not sumo. Elbows high throughout. · 2 min rest

exercises.name: Clean + 3 Front Squats
exercises.note: Hold the front rack actively on all 3 squats — elbows forward, no dropping · 2 min rest

exercises.name: Split Jerk (3s hold in split)
exercises.note: BACK FOOT: heel UP, toes pointing forward — not sideways. Hold 3 seconds and check before standing. · 2 min rest

exercises.name: Front Squat
exercises.note: Active front rack every rep — elbows chase the wall in front of you · 2 min rest

exercises.name: Push Press
exercises.note: Reinforce overhead position — drives the elbow-up habit · 75s rest

exercises.name: Overhead Squat
exercises.note: Arms locked OUT before you descend — press the bar into the sky, don't let it sit on a bent arm · 90s rest

exercises.name: Muscle Snatch
exercises.note: Arms lock overhead BEFORE the bar stops moving — if you're catching bent, you turned over too slow · 60s rest

exercises.name: Hang Snatch (above knee)
exercises.note: Starting above the knee means you CANNOT stop at the knee — use this. Accelerate to the hip. · 2 min rest

exercises.name: Snatch Pull (no stop at knee)
exercises.note: Bar sweeps CONTINUOUSLY off the floor — no speed bump at the knee. Think "sweep to hip." · 90s rest

exercises.name: Back Squat
exercises.note: 2 min rest

exercises.name: Bent Over Row
exercises.note: Back strength for the pull · 75s rest

phases.title: BLOCK 2 — POWER & SPEED
phases.subtitle: Weeks 5–8 · 7/9–8/3 · Power variations force correct positions under speed

intent.title: Week 5 · 7/9–7/13
intent.text: Power block begins. Power clean forces a narrow, deliberate landing — no room to go wide in a power catch. Power snatch forces the arm lockout on contact. Every power variation is a position drill.

exercises.name: Tall Clean
exercises.note: Stand tall, arms long — drive elbows UP and around at the very top. Pure second-pull mechanics with no setup. · 45s rest

exercises.name: Power Clean
exercises.note: NARROW landing — hip-width only. Power catch forces you to land deliberately, not wide. Elbows forward and high. · 2 min rest

exercises.name: Power Clean + Split Jerk
exercises.note: Jerk: CHECK back foot before every single rep. Heel up, toe forward. Don't let it sneak back to flat. · 2.5 min rest

exercises.name: Jerk from Rack
exercises.note: Back foot drill — sole focus is heel up, toes 12 o'clock. Use these sets as pure foot placement reps. · 90s rest

exercises.name: Pause Front Squat
exercises.note: 3s pause — check elbow height and foot position while you're stuck in the hole · 2 min rest

exercises.name: Good Morning
exercises.note: Posterior chain · 75s rest

exercises.name: Tall Snatch
exercises.note: No legs — pure arm speed. Arms LOCK before the bar stops. If they're soft at the top here, they're soft in the full lift. · 45s rest

exercises.name: Power Snatch
exercises.note: Straight arms in the catch — press the bar overhead, don't receive it. Narrow feet. · 2 min rest

exercises.name: Hang Snatch (above knee)
exercises.note: Drill: above-knee start removes the option to slow at the knee. Be explosive from the very start. · 2 min rest

exercises.name: Snatch Pull (no stop at knee)
exercises.note: Supramaximal load — sweep through the knee, don't park there. Faster at the hip than at the floor. · 90s rest

exercises.name: Pause Back Squat
exercises.note: 3s pause · 2 min rest

exercises.name: Face Pull
exercises.note: Rear delt and external rotation — healthy shoulders for overhead work · 60s rest

intent.title: Week 6 · 7/16–7/20
intent.text: Load climbs in the power variations. The technical demands stay: narrow landing, elbows high in the clean rack, straight arms in the snatch receive, back foot every jerk.

exercises.name: Tall Clean
exercises.note: Stand tall, arms long — drive elbows UP and around at the very top. Pure second-pull mechanics with no setup. · 45s rest

exercises.name: Power Clean
exercises.note: NARROW landing — hip-width only. Power catch forces you to land deliberately, not wide. Elbows forward and high. · 2 min rest

exercises.name: Power Clean + Split Jerk
exercises.note: Jerk: CHECK back foot before every single rep. Heel up, toe forward. Don't let it sneak back to flat. · 2.5 min rest

exercises.name: Jerk from Rack

exercises.name: Pause Front Squat

exercises.name: Good Morning
exercises.note: Posterior chain · 75s rest

exercises.name: Power Snatch

exercises.name: Snatch Pull (no stop at knee)
exercises.note: Supramaximal load — sweep through the knee, don't park there. Faster at the hip than at the floor. · 90s rest

exercises.name: Pause Back Squat
exercises.note: 3s pause · 2 min rest

exercises.name: Face Pull
exercises.note: Rear delt and external rotation — healthy shoulders for overhead work · 60s rest

intent.title: Week 7 · 7/23–7/27
intent.text: Peak of the power block. Heaviest pulls of the cycle. The speed built here carries directly into the competition lift block.

exercises.name: Face Pull
exercises.note: Rear delt and external rotation — healthy shoulders for overhead work · 60s rest

intent.title: Week 8 · 7/30–8/3
intent.text: Deload. Maintain sharpness. Entering the competition lift block next week.

phases.title: BLOCK 3 — COMPETITION BUILDS
phases.subtitle: Weeks 9–12 · 8/6–8/31 · Wave loading to max · 1RM test on 8/31

intent.title: Week 9 · 8/6–8/10
intent.text: Wave loading on competition lifts. Every rep is a check on all five technical points. The waves give you multiple exposures to heavier loads in one session.

exercises.name: Clean & Jerk — Wave 1
exercises.note: 3 min rest · elbows forward in the rack, back foot on every jerk

exercises.name: Clean & Jerk — Wave 2
exercises.note: 3 min rest · elbows forward in the rack, back foot on every jerk

exercises.name: Clean & Jerk — Wave 3
exercises.note: 3 min rest · elbows forward in the rack, back foot on every jerk

exercises.name: Jerk from Rack
exercises.note: Continue the back foot drill on every jerk · 3 min rest

exercises.name: Front Squat
exercises.note: 2 min rest

exercises.name: Push Press
exercises.note: 75s rest

exercises.name: Bent Over Row
exercises.note: 75s rest

exercises.name: Snatch — Wave 1
exercises.note: 2.5 min rest · arms locked before the bar stops, every rep

exercises.name: Snatch — Wave 2
exercises.note: 2.5 min rest · arms locked before the bar stops, every rep

exercises.name: Snatch — Wave 3
exercises.note: 2.5 min rest · arms locked before the bar stops, every rep

exercises.name: Snatch Pull
exercises.note: Keep the drill — no stop at the knee · 90s rest

exercises.name: Back Squat
exercises.note: 2 min rest

intent.title: Week 10 · 8/13–8/17
intent.text: Intensity climbs. Waves push toward 90%+. The technical work is now habit — trust it under heavier loads.

exercises.name: Clean & Jerk — Wave 1
exercises.note: 3 min rest · all cues apply

exercises.name: Clean & Jerk — Wave 2
exercises.note: 3 min rest · all cues apply

exercises.name: Clean & Jerk — Wave 3
exercises.note: 3 min rest · all cues apply

exercises.name: Jerk from Rack
exercises.note: Back foot check every rep · 3 min rest

exercises.name: Front Squat
exercises.note: 2 min rest

exercises.name: Push Press
exercises.note: 75s rest

exercises.name: Bent Over Row
exercises.note: 75s rest

exercises.name: Snatch — Wave 1
exercises.note: 2.5 min rest · trust the arm speed

exercises.name: Snatch — Wave 2
exercises.note: 2.5 min rest · trust the arm speed

exercises.name: Snatch — Wave 3
exercises.note: 2.5 min rest · trust the arm speed

exercises.name: Snatch Pull
exercises.note: Keep the drill — no stop at the knee · 90s rest

exercises.name: Back Squat
exercises.note: 2 min rest

intent.title: Week 11 · 8/20–8/24
intent.text: Near-max waves. Heaviest competition lifts of the cycle. All 5 technical cues apply on every rep. This is the peak before the taper.

exercises.name: Clean & Jerk — Wave 1
exercises.note: 3 min rest · every technical habit you've built shows here

exercises.name: Clean & Jerk — Wave 2
exercises.note: 3 min rest · every technical habit you've built shows here

exercises.name: Clean & Jerk — Wave 3
exercises.note: 3 min rest · every technical habit you've built shows here

exercises.name: Jerk from Rack
exercises.note: Trust the position — you've drilled it · 3 min rest

exercises.name: Front Squat
exercises.note: 2 min rest

exercises.name: Push Press
exercises.note: 75s rest

exercises.name: Bent Over Row
exercises.note: 75s rest

exercises.name: Snatch — Wave 1
exercises.note: 3 min rest · commit to the catch

exercises.name: Snatch — Wave 2
exercises.note: 3 min rest · commit to the catch

exercises.name: Snatch — Wave 3
exercises.note: 3 min rest · commit to the catch

exercises.name: Snatch Pull
exercises.note: Keep the drill — no stop at the knee · 90s rest

exercises.name: Back Squat
exercises.note: 2 min rest

intent.title: Week 12 · 8/27–8/31 🏆
intent.text: Test week. Thursday is opener feel on both lifts. Monday is 1RM day: Snatch, C&J, and Back Squat. Everything you've built — elbows, foot width, arm lock, pull speed, back foot — shows up here.

exercises.name: Clean & Jerk — Wave 1
exercises.note: 2 min rest · easy, feel the lift

exercises.name: Clean & Jerk — Wave 2
exercises.note: 2 min rest · easy, feel the lift

exercises.name: Jerk from Rack
exercises.note: Opener feel. Easy jerk. · 2 min rest

exercises.name: Front Squat
exercises.note: 2 min rest

exercises.name: Push Press
exercises.note: 75s rest

exercises.name: Snatch
exercises.note: Opener feel. Lock arms before the bar stops. · 3 min rest

exercises.name: Snatch — 1RM
exercises.note: Work up: 85%×1, 90%×1, 93%×1, then go · 4 min between attempts

exercises.name: Clean & Jerk — 1RM
exercises.note: Work up: 85%×1, 90%×1, then go · 4 min between attempts · all cues: elbows, width, back foot

exercises.name: Back Squat — 1RM
exercises.note: 85%×1, 90%×1, 95%×1, then attempt · 4 min rest
